profile.name: Lisset Dayana
profile.photoAlt: Professional portrait of Lisset Dayana Gómez Pérez
profile.photoText: Top graduate of her class, scientific merit award recipient, with experience in teaching, research, customer service and operations.
profile.summary: Radiochemist and early-career researcher with experience in university teaching, computational chemistry, molecular modeling and professional service. She combines scientific rigor, critical thinking and humane communication for academic, scientific and organizational environments.

professionalProfile: Scientific professional with a foundation in radiochemistry, analytical chemistry, quantum chemistry and molecular modeling. She has contributed to projects studying materials, bioactive systems and nanoscopic processes through classical methods, quantum methods and data analysis tools. Her teaching and service experience helps her translate complex knowledge into clear, organized and useful communication.

researchProjects.title: Modeling of nanoscopic structures and processes for materials and bioactivity
researchProjects.description: Participation in research at the Faculty of Chemistry focused on modeling chemically and biologically relevant systems, with emphasis on prediction, structural analysis and molecular-scale property interpretation.

researchProjects.title: Computational evaluation of compounds with potential biological activity
researchProjects.description: Application of computational methods to study ligand-receptor interactions, pharmacokinetic properties and molecular descriptors useful in early-stage research.
results: Communication of findings in scientific events

details: Higher education teaching in complex scientific subjects.
Class preparation, assessment and academic support.
Rigorous and clear communication for science students.
Modeling of nanoscopic structures and processes.
Application of classical, quantum and data-based methods.
Work focused on materials and bioactivity.

experience.organization: Eco-Hotel, Shopping Center and Recanto da Peixada
details: Direct customer service in multicultural contexts.
Operational organization, registration, support and service.
Work under pressure with warm communication.
Practical process design for local service.
Complementary cultural training for communication and professional judgment.

publications: Scientific abstract presented at chemistry and open science academic events.
Academic support materials for quantum chemistry teaching.
Editable space for DOI, articles, posters or conference proceedings.

contact.availability: Open to opportunities in research, teaching, laboratories, academic management, reception, administrative support and public service.
